other_observers: Bill Kaempfer; I was the original observer.
description: A small loon with overall gray plumage with a paler throat and flanks. The bill was small and gray and was always held up at a ~20 degree angle, never held horizontally. There were small white marks on the back-not pale fringes to the feathers but small white spots. The flanks were evenly white. When first seen, the bird was sleeping. The long body was particularly loon-like, and the pale flanks and pale gray color overall indicated that it was a Red-throated Loon. As soon as it lifted its head, the distinct bill size and position were apparent. There were no dark wedges on the side of the neck, only diffuse gray feathering.Behaviors: For the first couple of minutes the bird was sleeping. It eventually woke up and moved around a bit. It spent some time preening and flapped its wings on two occasions. When it was actively feeding, it sat low in the water, at times having its upper back submerged so just its rear and head/neck were above water. It caught a large (~4"), thin fish at one point which it swallowed whole. When it was relaxing/preening, it would roll over on its side exposing a bright white belly.Call: none heardPlumage: 1st cycle
